beats.narration_text: Ahoj. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of sinc² → fermi's golden rule. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of sinc² → fermi's golden rule

beats.narration_text: Sinc² → Fermi's Golden Rule is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Transition probability P(δ,T) = (|V_fi|/ħ)² · sin²(δT/2)/(δ/2)² — as T→∞, width→0, area→2πT, density→2π·δ(δ). This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Sinc² → Fermi's Golden Rule

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Sinc² → Fermi's Golden Rule

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Sinc² → Fermi's Golden Rule. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: T slider:. Verify: Transition probability P(δ,T) = (|V_fi|/ħ)² · sin²(δT/2)/(δ/2)² — as T→∞, width→0, area→2πT, density→2π·δ(δ). Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Sinc² → Fermi's Golden Rule. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: T slider:. Verify: Transition probability P

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Sinc² → Fermi's Golden Rule — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from vol3-sinc-squared-to-golden-rule.html

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Show the core physics loop from vol3-sinc-squared-to-golden-rule.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: sinc² → fermi's golden rule. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag T slider: from 1 to 100. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of T slider: from 1 to 100

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of T slider: from 1 to 100

beats.narration_text: Transition probability P(δ,T) = (|V_fi|/ħ)² · sin²(δT/2)/(δ/2)² — as T→∞, width→0, area→2πT, density→2π·δ(δ). Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of sinc² → fermi's golden rule. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Sinc² → Fermi's Golden Rule simulation and add a second slider that controls T slider:. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Sinc² → Fermi's Golden Rule simulation and add a second slider that controls T slider:. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
